name: Jira (Atlassian Cloud)

jiraRestApi.notes: Jira Cloud REST API (v3) handles project/issue management. User provisioning is done via the SCIM API at the organization level.

rateLimits.notes: Rate limits apply per API token. Specific limits are not publicly detailed for the SCIM API. Jira REST API uses a 'token bucket' algorithm.

scim.authentication: Bearer token (Directory API token generated in Atlassian admin)
scim.notes: SCIM provisioning requires Atlassian Guard (formerly Atlassian Access). Supports Users and Groups schemas. DELETE deactivates rather than permanently deleting.

sdk.notes: jira.js is the most popular community TypeScript/JavaScript SDK for Jira Cloud REST API. There is no official Atlassian npm SDK for the SCIM API; use direct HTTP calls.

webhooks.registrationEndpoint: POST /rest/api/3/webhook (dynamic) or via Jira admin UI (static)
webhooks.notes: Jira Cloud supports both static webhooks (configured in admin UI) and dynamic webhooks (registered via REST API by Connect/Forge apps). User lifecycle events may be limited to Atlassian organization-level events.

sandbox.notes: Free Jira Cloud instances available (up to 10 users). Atlassian Marketplace developers get free development instances. Full sandbox environments require Premium or Enterprise plans.

needs_verification: Exact user lifecycle webhook event names (user_created, user_updated, user_deleted) - may only be available via Atlassian organization audit log, not Jira webhooks
OAuth scopes needed specifically for SCIM provisioning (SCIM uses directory API tokens, not OAuth)
